summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates host secrets to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: On npm install, postinstall.js runs a gated payload that targets CTF/grader-style worker environments (hostname matching a 12-hex pattern, cwd of /app/node_modules, presence of /app/Cargo.toml or /app/package.json) and explicitly skips installs from Chinese registry mirrors (npmmirror, tencent, taobao, cnpm, huawei, ustc) to evade mirror-based scanners. When the gate matches, the script shells out to read installer-side secrets — /root/.npmrc, /usr/etc/npmrc, /run/secrets, /var/run/secrets, every /proc/<pid>/environ, and a recursive grep for HTB{...}, flag{...}, FLAG, SECRET, TOKEN, and AWS across /root, /opt, /usr/local/bin, /run, /var/run, and /tmp — base64-encodes the result, and PUTs it over plain HTTP to http://154.57.164.71:30782/api/modules/ECT-839201. The hardcoded bare-IP destination, plaintext transport, environment-fingerprint gating, and mirror-based evasion are an unambiguous credential/flag exfiltration pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
